statement: Standalone AI-to-C subscriptions are difficult because heavy users create variable inference costs while casual users churn.
would_strengthen: Multiple standalone consumer AI products show weak retention or negative gross margin.
Usage caps or throttling are introduced after heavy usage growth.
Paid conversion remains low despite high free usage.
would_weaken: A standalone consumer AI company shows durable paid retention and positive gross margin.
Inference cost declines enough to support generous usage at consumer subscription prices.
would_contradict: Several independent AI-to-C products sustain growth, retention, and profitability without platform subsidy.

statement: The strongest consumer AI businesses will be hybrid models combining subscription with bundling, ads, enterprise pull-through, marketplace, or ecosystem retention.
would_strengthen: Successful AI-to-C products add second revenue lines.
Pure subscription products pivot toward ads, marketplace, usage credits, or enterprise.
would_weaken: Pure subscription products outperform hybrid products on retention and margin.
would_contradict: The category standardizes around profitable pure subscriptions.

statement: Large companies are advantaged because they can subsidize consumer usage using infrastructure, distribution, identity/data, bundling, and strategic patience.
would_strengthen: Platform products bundle AI into existing subscriptions or default surfaces.
Standalone challengers struggle with acquisition cost or compute cost.
would_weaken: Standalone challengers grow efficiently without platform distribution.
would_contradict: Standalone challengers consistently beat platform AI products on retention, growth, and monetization.

statement: Tiered usage plans are a natural response to margin pressure because they segment willingness to pay and control heavy-user cost.
would_strengthen: Multiple consumer AI products introduce usage tiers, credits, caps, or priority queues.
Tiering is linked to advanced models, media generation, agent tasks, or heavy compute features.
would_weaken: Products remove usage limits while improving margin.
would_contradict: Unlimited flat-rate consumer AI becomes profitable across categories.

statement: Consumer/business convergence makes AI-to-C more sustainable when AI attaches to broader productivity subscriptions.
would_strengthen: Consumer AI products drive measurable upgrades to productivity bundles.
Business features subsidize or reinforce consumer surfaces.
would_weaken: Consumers resist bundled AI upsells or churn from AI-heavy bundles.
would_contradict: Standalone consumer-only AI products outperform productivity-bundled AI.
